steps.title: Identify the criterion for tall
steps.content: A perennial shrub is classified as tall if its height exceeds 120 centimeters. To represent a tall species, an inequality must include at least some possible heights where $h \gt  120$.

steps.title: Find the range that includes heights greater than 120
steps.content:
Check the maximum value in each inequality:
  • Choice A — $94 \lt h \lt 94$ impossible
  • Choice B — $94 \lt h \lt 117$ all values are less than 120
  • Choice C — $94 \lt h \lt 120$ all values satisfy $h \lt 120$
  • Choice D — $94 \lt h \lt 168$ includes values greater than 120 (between 120 and 168)
Only the matching choice's range permits tall plants. D is correct.
